steps.goal: Identify and extract the three distinct facts provided in the parent instructions. These facts are: (1) Open-Eywa treats the node as the durable unit of work. (2) Mission runs are stored in mission folders. (3) Testing improves reliability.
steps.title: Extract Facts

steps.goal: Draft the first part of an operator-style note, focusing solely on the fact that "Open-Eywa treats the node as the durable unit of work." The output should be a concise, standalone sentence or two.
steps.title: Draft Part 1 of Note

steps.goal: Draft the second part of an operator-style note, focusing solely on the fact that "Mission runs are stored in mission folders." The output should be a concise, standalone sentence or two.
steps.title: Draft Part 2 of Note

steps.goal: Draft the third part of an operator-style note, focusing solely on the fact that "Testing improves reliability." The output should be a concise, standalone sentence or two.
steps.title: Draft Part 3 of Note

steps.goal: Combine the three drafted parts from Step 2, Step 3, and Step 4 into a single, coherent, short operator-style note. Ensure the note flows well and maintains a consistent tone.
steps.title: Synthesize Final Note

steps.goal: Review the synthesized operator-style note for clarity, conciseness, and adherence to the "operator-style" requirement. Make any necessary minor edits to improve readability and impact without adding new information.
steps.title: Review and Refine Note
